title: Birthday Trip in Budapest
description: Spent my birthday wandering between the Buda and Pest sides of the Danube. The city has this faded grandeur to it — palaces, thermal baths, and trams rattling past — and somehow it all just works.
coverAlt: View across the Danube to Buda Castle on a clear afternoon, with the green dome and palace walls catching the light

images.alt: Buda Castle seen across the Danube under a bright blue sky, the green dome of the Royal Palace rising above the fortress walls with construction cranes in the distance
images.caption: First proper look at Buda Castle from the Pest side. Hard to believe this is just across a river.

images.alt: A yellow Budapest tram on the riverside line beside an ornate cast-iron lamppost, the Danube and Pest skyline stretching into the distance
images.caption: Riding the tram along the Danube — supposedly one of the most scenic tram rides in the world. Hard to argue.

images.alt: The Széchenyi Chain Bridge lit up at blue hour, golden lights tracing the suspension cables and reflecting off the dark surface of the Danube
images.caption: Chain Bridge at blue hour. Worth the wait.
